rental_information.name: Michael Anthony Reynolds
rental_information.relationships_to_self: Total of 2 occupants. 1) Self – primary applicant, Michael Anthony Reynolds. 2) Domestic partner/fiancée – Emily Rose Hart, age 34, will be a co-occupant but not listed as a co-applicant on this particular form because she is filling out a separate application as requested by the property manager. No children or other dependents will be residing in the unit at this time.
rental_information.list_any_pets: We have one indoor-only cat: Name: Luna. Species: Domestic house cat. Approximate age: 4 years old. Weight: about 10 lbs. She is spayed, microchipped, and fully up to date on vaccinations (rabies, FVRCP). She is strictly an indoor cat, litter-box trained, and we use a covered litter box with clumping litter that we clean daily. She has a scratching post and cardboard scratchers that we bring with us and we use nail caps occasionally to prevent furniture damage. No dogs, reptiles, or other animals. We do not plan to acquire additional pets during the lease term without first obtaining written permission from management.
rental_information.breeds: Luna is a short-haired domestic shorthair cat with a grey and white tuxedo pattern. She does not have a specific registered pedigree breed; she is a mixed-breed domestic shorthair adopted from the Multnomah County Animal Services shelter.

residence_history.reason_for_moving: The main reasons for moving are: (1) Our current building was recently sold to a new owner who has notified tenants that substantial renovations will be taking place over the next year, including repiping and replacement of windows, which will involve significant noise, periodic water shutoffs, and potential temporary relocations from units. (2) The new ownership has already announced an upcoming rent increase that will push our current unit above what we feel is reasonable for the space and condition (our rent would go from $1,650 to $1,925 per month). (3) We are looking for a slightly larger, quieter unit with better sound insulation and on-site parking, as my partner has started working from home full-time and needs a more peaceful environment for video calls. (4) The new property you manage is closer to my office downtown and to Emily’s yoga studio, which will reduce our commute times and transportation costs. In short, we are looking for a more stable, better-managed building with amenities that fit our current life stage, while avoiding a steep rent hike and disruptive construction.
residence_history.current_landlord_name: Greenwood Property Management, LLC (Attn: Sarah Collins, Property Manager)
